info.name: Car Showroom Management System
info.description:
Complete API test collection. Run folders top-to-bottom in order — each folder builds on the previous one.

📋 PREREQUISITES
• Server running: npm run dev
• Database migrated: npm run migrate
• Admin seeded: npm run seed
• Enable cookie jar in Postman (Settings → Use cookie jar)

🔑 DEFAULT CREDENTIALS
• Admin: admin@example.com / admin123
• Employee: auto-created in folder 2

⚡ AUTO-SAVED VARIABLES
After each create request, IDs are automatically saved as collection variables (car_id, customer_id, rental_id, purchase_id, employee_id). No manual copying needed.

item.name: 0. Health Check
item.description: Verify the server is running before running any other requests.

request.description: Returns {status: 'ok', timestamp: '...'}. If this fails, the server is not running.

item.name: 1. Authentication
item.description: Start here. Login as admin to get a session cookie. Postman stores it automatically.

request.description: Authenticates with admin credentials. Session cookie is automatically stored by Postman.
Returns the currently authenticated user. Requires active session.

item.name: 2. User Management (Admin Only)
item.description:
Create and manage employee accounts. Only admin can access these endpoints.

⚠️ Run 'Delete Employee' AFTER completing folder 8 (Permission Tests), since it needs an active employee.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('employee_id', json.id); console.log('Saved employee_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Creates a new employee user. Role must be 'employee' — admin cannot create another admin via API. Saves employee_id variable.
Returns all users (admin + employees) with a total count.

exec: pm.test('All results are employees', () => { pm.response.json().users.forEach(u => pm.expect(u.role).to.equal('employee')); });

request.description: Filter users by role. Also supports ?is_active=true|false.
Fetch a single user by UUID. Uses auto-saved {{employee_id}}.
Update full_name and/or is_active. Partial update supported.
⚠️ Run AFTER folder 8 (Permission Tests). Sets is_active=false. The employee will no longer be able to log in.

item.name: 3. Customers
item.description: Manage customer records. Accessible by admin and employees.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('customer_id', json.id); console.log('Saved customer_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Create a customer. At least one of phone or email is required. Saves customer_id variable.
Paginated list of all customers.
Search by name, phone, or email (case-insensitive ILIKE match).
Fetch a single customer by UUID.
Partial update. Only provided fields are changed.

item.name: 4. Cars — Admin Endpoints
item.description: Manage the car inventory. Accessible by admin and employees.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('car_id', json.id); console.log('Saved car_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Creates the main test car. Saves car_id variable. VIN is optional but must be unique.

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('car2_id', json.id); console.log('Saved car2_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Second car used in the Purchases folder. Saves car2_id variable.
Returns all cars regardless of is_published status. Admin view.
Returns car details plus its images array.
Partial update of any car fields.
Add an image. Only one primary image allowed per car (enforced by unique index).
Add a second image with a different sort_order.
Toggle car visibility on the public listing. After this, the car appears on GET /cars.

item.name: 5. Cars — Public Endpoints
item.description: No authentication required. Only shows is_published=true cars. Run after publishing a car in folder 4.

exec: pm.test('All cars have images', () => { pm.response.json().cars.forEach(c => pm.expect(c).to.have.property('images')); });

request.description: Public endpoint — no auth needed. Returns only published cars with their images.
Filter public cars by brand, model, year range, sale price range, or rent price range.
Public endpoint. Returns full car details including all images.

item.name: 6. Rentals
item.description: Full rental lifecycle: create → confirmed → active (car becomes rented) → completed (car becomes available again).

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('rental_id', json.id); console.log('Saved rental_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Creates rental. Checks date overlap, snapshots rent_price_per_day, calculates total (7 days inclusive = 7 × 1900 = 13300 THB).
Returns all rentals with joined car and customer info.
Filter by status: pending | confirmed | active | completed | cancelled. Also supports car_id, customer_id, start_date_from, start_date_to.
Returns rental with joined car_brand, car_model, customer_name details.
Transition: pending → confirmed. No side effect on car status.

exec: console.log('Side effect: car status should now be rented. Check GET /admin/cars/{{car_id}}');

request.description: Transition: confirmed → active. SIDE EFFECT: cars.status = 'rented'.
Confirms the side effect: car status changed to 'rented' when rental went active.

exec: console.log('Side effect: car status should now be available again.');

request.description: Transition: active → completed. SIDE EFFECT: cars.status = 'available'.
Confirms the side effect: car status returned to 'available' after rental completed.

item.name: 7. Purchases
item.description: Purchase lifecycle: create → paid (car becomes sold). Uses car2_id (Honda Civic).

exec: if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('purchase_id', json.id); console.log('Saved purchase_id:', json.id); }

request.description: Creates purchase for the Honda Civic. Snapshots sale_price from cars table. Saves purchase_id.
Returns all purchases with joined car and customer info. Filter by ?car_id=, ?customer_id=, ?status=.
Returns purchase details with car and customer join info.

item.name: Mark as Paid → Car Becomes Sold

exec: console.log('ATOMIC TRANSACTION: car2 status is now sold. Test this in folder 9.');

request.description: CRITICAL: Uses DB transaction to atomically set purchase=paid AND car=sold. Cannot be reversed (no paid→pending).
Confirms the atomic side effect: Honda Civic is now marked as sold.

item.name: 8. Permission Tests (Employee Access)
item.description:
Tests role-based access control. Login as employee, verify they CAN access cars/customers/rentals but CANNOT access user management.

⚠️ This temporarily switches session to employee. The last request re-logs in as admin.

request.description: Switch session to employee. Subsequent requests run as employee.

exec: pm.test('Status 200 - Employee can access cars', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Employee has full access to car management. Expect 200.

exec: pm.test('Status 200 - Employee can access customers', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

request.description: Employee has full access to customer management. Expect 200.
Employee is blocked from user management. Expect 403 Forbidden.
Employee cannot create users. Expect 403 Forbidden.
Re-login as admin for subsequent tests.

item.name: 9. Business Rule Validations
item.description: Test that the system correctly enforces business rules and returns proper error codes.

request.description: Honda Civic (car2_id) was sold in folder 7. Attempting to rent it must return 400.
Honda Civic (car2_id) is sold. Attempting to purchase it must return 400.

exec: console.log('Now run the next request to test overlap detection.');

request.description: Creates a rental Jun 1–10. Status is 'pending' so overlap check will fire on the next request.
Jun 8–15 overlaps with the pending Jun 1–10 rental. Must return 409.

NOTE: This uses employee_id here for the actual test. To test deleting the actual admin, you would need the admin's UUID. The admin route protects against role=admin deletion. Run 'Delete Employee' from folder 2 to soft-delete the employee.
The rental from folder 6 is 'completed'. Trying to go back to 'pending' must fail.

item.name: 10. Cleanup
item.description: Run these at the very end.

item.name: Delete Employee (if not done in folder 2)

request.description: Soft-deletes the employee created in folder 2.
Destroys the session. Subsequent authenticated requests will return 401.
Confirms the session was destroyed by logout.
